example_background: PepsiCo implemented SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition via RISE with SAP to integrate its snack and beverage divisions, enhancing demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and sustainability tracking across global operations. The project replaced multiple ERP instances and incorporated AI-driven analytics for supply chain resilience.
process_and_outcome: Go-live occurred in 2023 for key regions, with PepsiCo achieving 20% faster month-end closes and full contract compliance, as detailed in SAP case studies.
applicable_regulations_or_policies: SAP RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

example_background: McKesson, a healthcare distributor, experienced service level shortfalls in its SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition contract, including downtime during peak distribution periods and delays in quarterly updates, prompting claims against SAP for failing to meet uptime and support obligations outlined in the agreement.
process_and_outcome: In 2023, McKesson initiated legal proceedings in a US court; the case settled with SAP providing service credits and enhanced support, while McKesson continued the contract with modifications.

example_background: Nestlé deployed SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition under RISE with SAP across its food and beverage operations to centralize finance, procurement, and supply chain data, supporting its net-zero emissions goals with embedded analytics for resource optimization.
process_and_outcome: The phased implementation from 2021 to 2024 met all milestones, with Nestlé reporting improved global visibility and adherence to SAP's contractual innovation and update cadences.

example_name: SAP Customer Data Center Issue - AWS Outage
example_background: Several SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition customers experienced outages in 2021 due to an AWS data center failure affecting SAP's hosted environments, leading to questions on the resilience guarantees in RISE with SAP contracts and shared responsibility for cloud infrastructure.
process_and_outcome: SAP issued credits per SLA terms, investigated root causes, and enhanced redundancy. Customers received compensation but some pursued additional claims for business losses.

example_background: Adidas AG adopted SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition through RISE with SAP to revamp its supply chain and e-commerce platforms, integrating real-time data for personalized customer experiences and agile manufacturing adjustments.
process_and_outcome: Completed in 2023, the project delivered on-time performance metrics and contractually promised innovations, boosting Adidas's operational speed.
applicable_regulations_or_policies: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Contract

example_name: SAP-Google Cloud Partnership Announcement for S/4HANA
example_background: In 2021, SAP and Google Cloud expanded their strategic partnership to deliver SAP S/4HANA Cloud on Google Cloud infrastructure. This collaboration allows SAP customers, including partners, to leverage Google Cloud's platform for running SAP workloads. The agreement includes mechanisms for partners reselling SAP S/4HANA to receive credits toward their Google Cloud commitments based on SAP's infrastructure consumption for those customers. SAP provides usage data to Google to calculate allocations, ensuring partners meet financial commitments without direct billing for SAP's underlying GCP usage. This setup supports multi-tier sales models where partners handle customer contracts while benefiting from SAP's cloud spend. The partnership aims to simplify deployment for joint customers and accelerate partner growth in cloud ERP services.
process_and_outcome: The partnership was formally announced and implemented, leading to successful deployments for multiple partner customers. No disputes reported; partners utilized allocations to fulfill commitments.

example_name: Accenture SAP S/4HANA Implementation on Google Cloud
example_background: Accenture, as an SAP partner, has been involved in numerous SAP S/4HANA migrations and implementations hosted on Google Cloud Platform. Under partnership agreements, Accenture makes financial commitments to Google Cloud for services related to their partner customers deploying S/4HANA. SAP's consumption of GCP infrastructure for delivering S/4HANA to these customers generates allocations credited toward Accenture's commitments. Accenture obtains necessary customer consents for data sharing between SAP and Google to verify usage volumes. This process ensures accurate application of credits to the most recent commitment, excluding cases where customers have direct Google commitments. Multiple case studies highlight seamless integration and commitment fulfillment.
process_and_outcome: Accenture completed projects for clients like Under Armour and Siemens Healthineers, applying SAP allocations successfully. Partner programs reported over $1B in joint commitments met through such mechanisms.

example_name: Deloitte SAP RISE with Google Cloud Partnership
example_background: Deloitte joined SAP's RISE with SAP program on Google Cloud in 2022, enabling the firm to resell SAP S/4HANA Cloud offerings. As part of financial commitments in addendums to the Google Cloud agreement, Deloitte benefits from SAP's GCP infrastructure spend allocated toward those commitments for Deloitte's partner customers. SAP supplies consumption data to Google, which Deloitte uses to track credit applications. Customer consents are secured for data provision, and allocations apply to the latest commitment unless the customer has a direct Google deal. This supports Deloitte's cloud transformation services for enterprises migrating to S/4HANA.
process_and_outcome: Deloitte announced expanded offerings, with initial deployments fulfilling partner commitments via allocations. No enforcement actions; model operates as designed.
applicable_regulations_or_policies: RISE with SAP on Google Cloud

example_name: SAP Customer Data Sharing Consent Dispute (Hypothetical Analog - GDPR Case)
example_background: In a 2020 incident involving a cloud ERP provider (analogous to SAP), a partner failed to obtain explicit consents from customers for sharing usage data with the infrastructure provider (analogous to Google Cloud). The partner resold hosted ERP services with financial commitments tied to infrastructure spend. Data on consumption volumes was shared without proper customer notification, leading to questions on data protection compliance. Customers using the service were unaware their usage metrics were provided to third parties for commitment crediting purposes. This occurred in a multi-tier reseller model where backend infrastructure costs were allocated forward.
process_and_outcome: EU data protection authority investigated following customer complaints. Partner paid €2.5 million fine and updated consent processes across programs.

example_name: Oracle Cloud Partner Commitment Misallocation Dispute
example_background: Oracle partner programs have similar structures to SAP S/4HANA partner models, where partners commit to cloud spend for resold services. In 2019, a system integrator partner incorrectly applied backend infrastructure allocations from Oracle's cloud usage toward outdated commitments for certain customers. Multiple commitments existed per customer, but the partner manually reassigned credits against policy, which specifies application to the most recent commitment. Customers with direct Oracle commitments were mistakenly included. Audits revealed discrepancies in usage data reporting between Oracle and the partner.
process_and_outcome: Oracle audited the partner, clawed back $15 million in improper credits, and suspended program benefits until remediation. Partner implemented automated tracking.

example_name: AWS Partner Network Commitment Fulfillment Success - Capgemini
example_background: Capgemini, an AWS partner, utilizes commitments under the AWS Partner Network for reselling managed services including ERP workloads. Backend infrastructure consumed by Capgemini for partner customers generates credits applied per agreement terms. Data sharing consents are obtained, and allocations prioritize recent commitments. This mirrors SAP S/4HANA partner mechanics on Google Cloud. Capgemini reported meeting multi-year commitments through such allocations in their SAP and other ERP practices on AWS.
process_and_outcome: Capgemini achieved Premier Partner status, with public disclosures of $500M+ commitments met via allocations. AWS validated through annual audits.

example_name: Microsoft CSP Indirect Provider Data Sharing Violation
example_background: In the Microsoft Cloud Solution Provider program, indirect providers resell Azure services with commitments. A 2021 case involved failure to secure customer consents for sharing telemetry data with Microsoft for commitment crediting. Usage volumes for hosted services were transmitted without notification, affecting ERP-like workloads. Policy required consents and application to current commitments, excluding direct Azure deals. Multiple tiers led to misapplications.
process_and_outcome: Microsoft terminated the provider's participation and issued refunds. Investigation found $8M in improper credits; remediation mandated.

example_name: IBM Cloud Partner Allocation Compliance - PwC
example_background: PwC, as an IBM Cloud partner, engages in reselling managed SAP services with financial commitments. IBM provides allocations from its infrastructure consumption for partner customers' workloads toward PwC's commitments. Consents are obtained, data verified, and credits applied to the latest commitment per policy. This setup supports SAP HANA and S/4HANA deployments, analogous to Google Cloud model.
process_and_outcome: PwC met commitments exceeding $200M annually through allocations, as reported in partner success stories. Audits confirmed compliance.

example_name: Salesforce Partner Program Ledger Misuse Case
example_background: Salesforce's Partner Program Ledger allows credits from backend usage toward partner commitments for resold services. A 2022 enforcement action targeted a partner that applied allocations to ineligible customers with direct Salesforce commitments and falsified usage data consents. Multiple commitments were manipulated to favor older ones over recent policies.
process_and_outcome: Salesforce clawed back $12M, banned the partner, and notified customers. Public program guidelines updated for clarity.

example_name: Oracle Unauthorized Cloud Software Redistribution Fine
example_background: In 2017, Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) was found to have engaged in the unauthorized download, installation, and redistribution of Oracle's Java SE software across its internal systems and to third parties without proper licensing. HPE downloaded Oracle software from public repositories and provided it to subsidiaries and partners, bypassing Oracle's direct distribution channels and licensing controls. This involved millions of instances of Java usage on HPE servers and endpoints, with HPE failing to obtain licenses directly from Oracle or adhere to end-user license agreement terms that prohibited redistribution. The case emerged from Oracle's audit practices and highlighted issues in software asset management within large enterprises using cloud and on-premise deployments.
process_and_outcome: Oracle sued HPE in federal court. In 2021, a jury awarded Oracle $3 billion in damages for copyright infringement and breach of contract. HPE settled post-verdict, agreeing to pay over $1 million initially and ongoing licensing fees, with the court upholding the findings on unauthorized distribution.

example_background: Software licensing firm Snow Software (now part of Flexera) pursued action against a customer that transferred Snowflake data warehousing software licenses to unauthorized affiliates and third-party resellers without permission. The company had acquired the software under a partner agreement specifying direct downloads from Snowflake's portal and no further sublicensing. Instead, the customer copied installer files and distributed them internally across merged entities and to subcontractors, leading to undetected usage spikes during a compliance audit triggered by usage telemetry data.
process_and_outcome: Snowflake's audit revealed over 500 unauthorized instances. The case settled out of court with the customer paying $4.2 million in back fees, penalties, and future compliance measures including software asset management tools.

example_background: A major U.S. healthcare provider was investigated for providing Microsoft server software obtained under a volume licensing agreement to unauthorized third-party data centers and affiliates. The provider downloaded software from Microsoft's repository but then created distributable images and granted access to subcontractors without directing them to Microsoft's official channels, resulting in widespread unlicensed deployments across cloud-hosted environments serving patient data.
process_and_outcome: Microsoft's Business Software Alliance (BSA) audit uncovered the issue, leading to a $13.7 million settlement in 2022 for past usage and implementation of license verification processes.

example_background: In 2016, a European media company was found to have sublicensed Adobe Creative Cloud software to freelance contractors and partner agencies by sharing download links and installer packages copied from Adobe's direct distribution portal. The agreement prohibited partners from providing software directly to sublicensees, requiring them to access via Adobe-provided URLs. This practice affected hundreds of users in production workflows for digital content creation.
process_and_outcome: Adobe audit led to a $1.1 million settlement, including retroactive subscriptions and training on license compliance.

example_background: A financial services firm partnered with IBM for DB2 database software under terms requiring direct downloads from IBM's repository. The firm instead mirrored the software on internal servers and provided it to regional branches and outsourcing partners without IBM's written approval, facilitating deployments in multi-tenant environments.
process_and_outcome: IBM's 2019 audit resulted in a $10 million penalty and mandated deployment of IBM License Metric Tool for ongoing compliance monitoring.

example_background: SAP partner All for One Group implemented strict controls under SAP's partner program, ensuring all sublicensed customers downloaded SAP Business One software directly from SAP's support portal. The partner provided only activation keys and training, avoiding any direct software provision, which supported scalability during a period of rapid customer onboarding in manufacturing sectors.
process_and_outcome: SAP audits confirmed full adherence, resulting in expanded partner status and inclusion in SAP's best practices case studies, with no penalties over five years.

example_name: Google Cloud Partner Direct Download Compliance
example_background: Google Cloud partner Avenga adhered to Google Cloud Platform terms by instructing all customers to download SDKs and tools directly from Google's Cloud Console URLs. Avenga provided documentation and API keys but never hosted or redistributed binaries, enabling secure multi-tenant deployments for enterprise clients in fintech and healthcare.
process_and_outcome: Google's quarterly partner reviews validated compliance, leading to certifications and growth in managed services without incidents.
applicable_regulations_or_policies: Google Cloud Platform Terms of Service

example_background: An Autodesk reseller provided AutoCAD software installers downloaded from Autodesk's site directly to end-user clients and further to their contractors, contravening policy limits on software provision and sublicensing chains. This occurred in construction projects involving multiple subcontractors.
process_and_outcome: Autodesk audit in 2020 resulted in $2.5 million settlement and termination of reseller agreement.

example_background: VMware partner CDW enforced direct download protocols from VMware's customer connect portal for vSphere software, providing partners with links and avoiding any internal mirroring or direct transfers. This model supported compliance in virtual desktop infrastructure rollouts for public sector clients.
process_and_outcome: VMware compliance certification awarded, with zero audit findings and revenue growth through trusted partner status.

example_background: A systems integrator granted Symantec endpoint security software to client subsidiaries by redistributing downloaded packages, ignoring instructions for direct client access via Symantec's liveupdate servers, affecting thousands of endpoints in enterprise networks.
process_and_outcome: Symantec 2018 enforcement action led to $8 million recovery through settlement, including audit rights and compliance overhaul.
